campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help fund youth recreation scholarships in the city. They also discussed extending an interim ordinance and moved forward with plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including approving a feasibility study and submitting a grant application.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth scholarship programs
Extension of interim ordinance #2326
Bicycle/pedestrian bridge project over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue
Grant application for Hamilton Avenue overcross planning studies

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to gather community input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council voted to move forward with the proposal and set it up for final approval at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for adoption at next council meeting

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focusing on formalizing previous meeting records and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several agreements and projects, including a contract update with the company that maintains the airport's baggage system, new rules to protect affordable housing when subsidies end or buildings face foreclosure, and planning work for the city's storm sewer system. The council also took action on a FEMA-funded family camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage system contract renewal with Vanderlande Industries
New protections for affordable housing when government subsidies expire
Storm sewer system master planning and design agreement
FEMA San José Family Camp project funding and implementation

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026, where they reviewed the city's spending plans for infrastructure projects in the upcoming fiscal year. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program budget and planned infrastructure projects
Teen Month proclamation
National Volunteer Week recognition

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and recognized two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting focused on raising awareness about these community initiatives and their significance to Sunnyvale residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community awareness and celebration
City Council regular session proceedings

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop to teach residents how to use OpenGov, a tool that shows where the city's money comes from and how it's spent. The council also reminded people that if they try to influence city decisions, they may need to register as a lobbyist and report what they're doing.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget information now more accessible to the public
Lobbyist registration requirements under Cupertino Municipal Code Chapter 2.100
How to report lobbying activities to the city

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 covered important legal information for residents. If you disagree with a City decision (like a permit denial or zoning ruling), you have 90 days from when that decision is final to file a lawsuit, unless state law allows you less time.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge City decisions in court
Legal disclosure requirements for City meetings
Rules governing quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure compliance

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including reviewing updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plan for 2025-2030, considering changes to how council vacancies are filled, and approving meeting minutes. Council members also submitted new requests for items to be studied or acted on in future meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the 5-year housing and community development plan (2025-2030)
Changes to the council vacancy ordinance (how empty council seats get filled)
New workplan requests from individual council members
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a proposal to upgrade the historic status of a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is requesting to raise the building's historic designation from Category 4 to Category 2, which would allow them to build an additional 5,000 square feet of space as a bonus for preserving and seismically upgrading the historic structure.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade proposal
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for historic preservation
Seismic safety improvements to existing building
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule updates
Downtown Commercial zone development
